translations.### Xcode 直接使用 Swift 撰寫 Shell Script！\n\: ### Writing Shell Scripts Directly in Swift in Xcode!

translations.*P.S 可以看到程式碼出現 `localizedStrings()` 命名，懷: *P.S You might notice the method name `localizedStrings()` in the code, which suggests the author borrowed it from the localization checking tool logic and forgot to rename it XD*
translations.- **Build Error** ❌ **:**\n\n- [Asset Mi:
- **Build Error** ❌ **:**

- [Asset Missing] Items are referenced in the code but do not exist in the image asset catalog.

- **Build Warning** ⚠️ **:**

- [Asset Unused] Items exist in the image asset directory but are not used in the code.  
  *p.s If the image is loaded via a dynamic variable, the checker cannot detect it. You can add it to `ignoredUnusedNames` as an exception.*
translations.- **Build Error** ❌ **:**\n\n- [Duplicat:
- **Build Error** ❌ **:**

- [Duplication] Items duplicated in localization files

- [Unused Key] Items defined in the localization file but not used in the actual code

- [Missing] Items not defined in the localization files but actually used in the code

- [Redundant] Items that are redundant in this localization file compared to the main localization file

- [Missing Translation] The key exists in the main language file but is missing in this language file

- **Build Warning** ⚠️ **:**

- [Potentially Untranslated] This item is untranslated (same content as the base language file item)
translations.- Automatic Checks During Build\n\n- Aut:
- Automatic Checks During Build

- Automatic Localization File Formatting and Organization

- Check for Missing or Extra Localizations and the Base Language

- Check for Duplicate Localization Strings

- Check Untranslated Localization Strings

- Check Unused Localization Strings
translations.- Automatic Checks During Build\n\n- Che:
- Automatic Checks During Build

- Check missing images: name is referenced, but the image asset folder does not contain it

- Check for redundant images: names not used, but image asset directory exists

translations.EX: `\"Hi, %@ welcome to my app\"、\"Hell: EX: `"Hi, %@ welcome to my app"`, `"Hello World!"` **<- These strings cannot be matched**
translations.[Asset Missing] 項目在程式內有呼叫使用，但圖片資源目錄內沒有出現: [Asset Missing] Items are called in the code but do not appear in the image asset catalog
translations.[Asset Unused] 項目在程式內未使用，但圖片資源目錄內有出現\n*p:
[Asset Unused] Items are not used in the code but appear in the image asset directory.  
*p.s If the image is provided by a dynamic variable, the checker cannot detect it. You can add it to `ignoredUnusedNames` as an exception.*
translations.[Missing Translation] 項目在主要語系檔有，但在此語系檔缺漏: [Missing Translation] The key exists in the main language file but is missing in this language file
translations.[Missing] 項目在語系檔案內未定義，但實際程式中有使用到\n: [Missing] Items not defined in the localization files but actually used in the code
translations.[Potentially Untranslated] 此項目未經翻譯（與主語系檔: [Potentially Untranslated] This item is untranslated (same content as the base language file item)
translations.[Redundant] 項目在此語系檔相較於主要語系檔是多餘的\n: [Redundant] Items that are redundant in this localization file compared to the main localization file
translations.[Unused Key] 項目在語系檔案內有定義，但實際程式中未使用到\n: [Unused Key] Items defined in the localization file but not used in the actual code

[Download the Swift Script file for the tool](https://github.com/freshOS/Localize/blob/master/Localize.swift){:target="_blank"}
translations.^遍歷所有專案檔案並進行正則匹配的方法\n\n: ^Method to traverse all project files and perform regex matching
translations.build 專案時能自動檢查多語系有無錯誤、缺露、重複、Image Assets: Automatically check for errors, missing or duplicate entries in localizations, and missing Image Assets when building the project.
translations.一直以來都想找工具協助處理這方面的問題，之前是用 [iOSLocalizatio: I have always wanted a tool to help handle these issues. Previously, I used the Mac app [iOSLocalizationEditor](https://github.com/igorkulman/iOSLocalizationEditor){:target="_blank"}, but it is more like a localization file editor that reads and edits localization file content, without any automatic checking features.
translations.主要是調整 `NSLocalizedString` 方法後的匹配語句，變成取任意: The main adjustment is to the matching statement after the `NSLocalizedString` method, changing it to capture any string until a `"` appears to stop. You can also [click here](https://rubular.com/r/5eXvGy3svsAHyT){:target="_blank"} to customize it according to your needs.
translations.但檢查腳本需要使用 shell script 撰寫，因自己對 shell scr: But the check script needs to be written in shell script, and since I’m not very proficient with shell scripting, I tried searching online for existing scripts that fully meet my requirements but couldn’t find any. Just as I was about to give up, I suddenly thought:
translations.其他操作同語系檢查工具，這邊就不做贅述；最重要的事是也要 **記得調適完後要 c: Other operations are the same as the localization checking tool, so they won’t be repeated here; the most important thing is to **remember to compile it into an executable after debugging and change the run script content to the executable!**
translations.匹配 Swift/ObjC的 `NSLocalizedString()` 方法，: Matching Swift/ObjC's `NSLocalizedString()` method, this regular expression only matches strings in the format `"Home.Title"`; if we use complete sentences or include Format parameters, they will be mistakenly treated as [Unused Key].
translations.可以綜合參考以上的程式方法，自己打造想要的工具。\n\n: You can combine the above programming methods to create your own desired tools.
translations.可直接將設定參數帶在路徑上，參數1:專案目錄位置、參數2:圖片資源目錄位置；或跟: You can directly pass the configuration parameters in the path: Parameter 1 is the project directory location, Parameter 2 is the image assets directory location; or edit the parameter settings block at the top of `AssetChecker.swift` just like the localization checking tool:
translations.回頭看檢查腳本 `Localize.swift` 頂部設定區塊 patterns: Looking back at the first item in the patterns section of the `Localize.swift` script's top configuration block:
translations.因為自己手殘，時常在編輯多語系檔案時遺漏「;」導致 app build 出來語言: Because of my clumsiness, I often forget the ";" when editing localization files, causing language display errors in the app build. Additionally, as development progresses, the localization files grow larger, with duplicate and unused strings mixed in, making things very messy (the same issue applies to Image Assets).
translations.增加 `shell()` 執行 shell script，使用 `plutil: Add `shell()` to execute shell scripts, using `plutil -lint` to check the plist localization file format correctness. If there is an error or missing “;”, it will return an error; if no error, it returns `OK` as the validation result!
translations.完全符合我們的期望功能需求!! 並且他們使用 swift 撰寫，要客製化魔改都很: It fully meets our expected functional requirements!! Plus, since they are written in Swift, customizing and modifying them is very easy.
translations.導入 Localization 多語系及 Image Assets 缺漏檢查、使: Introducing Localization and Image Assets Missing Check, Using Swift to Build Shell Script
translations.我們可以依照自己的需求進行客製，例如把 error 換成 warning 或是拔: We can customize according to our needs, such as changing errors to warnings or disabling certain checks (e.g., Potentially Untranslated, Unused Key); since the script is in Swift, which we are all familiar with, there's no fear of breaking or messing it up!
translations.我們可以新增一條 patterns 設定、或更改原本的 patterns 成：\: We can add a new patterns setting or modify the existing patterns to:
translations.打開 terminal ，前往專案中檢查工具腳本所在目錄下執行：\n\n: Open the terminal and navigate to the directory containing the checker tool scripts in the project, then run:
translations.打開專案設定 → iOS Target → Build Phases →左上角「: Open Project Settings → iOS Target → Build Phases → "+" at the top left → New Run Script Phase → Paste the path in the Script content
Open Project Settings → iOS Target → Build Phases → Top left “+” → New Run Script Phase → Paste the script path in the Script content, e.g., `${SRCROOT}/Localize.swift`
translations.放到專案目錄下 EX: `${SRCROOT}/AssetChecker.swi: Place it in the project directory, e.g., `${SRCROOT}/AssetChecker.swift`
translations.放到專案目錄下 EX: `${SRCROOT}/Localize.swift`\: Place it in the project directory, e.g., `${SRCROOT}/Localize.swift`

translations.檢查的地方可加在 LocalizationFiles-&gt;process(): The check can be added after LocalizationFiles->process()-> `let location = singleLanguage…` around line 135, or refer to the complete modified version I provided at the end.
translations.此腳本僅針對語系檔做內容對應檢查，不會檢查檔案格式是否正確（是否有忘記加「 **: This script only checks the content correspondence of localization files and does not verify if the file format is correct (such as missing a "**;**"). You need to add this feature yourself if needed!
translations.然後再回頭到 Build Phases 更改 Script 內容路徑成執行檔\n: Then go back to Build Phases and change the Script content path to the executable file.
translations.由 [freshOS](https://freshos.github.io/){: Two checking tools written by the [freshOS](https://freshos.github.io/){:target="_blank"} team:
translations.當我們的 swift 檢查工具腳本都調試完成之後，要將其 **compile 成: After we finish debugging our Swift check tool scripts, we need to **compile them into executables to reduce build time**; otherwise, each build will recompile the scripts (this can save about 90% of the time).
translations.相對 shell script 來說更熟悉、掌握度更高！依照這個方向果然讓我找到: More familiar and proficient compared to shell scripts! Following this direction, I indeed found two existing tool scripts!
translations.相較於語系檢查腳本，這個腳本簡潔且重要的功能都有，很有參考價值！\n\n: Compared to the localization checking script, this script is simpler yet includes all the essential features, making it very valuable as a reference!

translations.要讓 build 時出現 Warning ⚠️：\n\n: To show a Warning ⚠️ during build:
translations.要達到我們的期望功能就要在 Build Phases 加入 Run Script: To achieve the desired functionality, you need to add a Run Script check in the Build Phases.
translations.語系檔自動排版、整理\n: Automatic Localization File Formatting and Organization
translations.這兩個檢查工具導入之後，我們在開發上就能更安心、更有效率並且減少冗餘；也因為這次: After integrating these two checking tools, we can develop with more confidence, greater efficiency, and less redundancy. This experience has been eye-opening, and in the future, any new build run script needs can be directly handled using the most familiar language, Swift!
